title: Fun YouTube Podcast: reframed 1080p sizzle cut and a branded graphics kit (lower-third + end-screen + vector logo)
one_line_ask: Turn the raw podcast footage into a reframed 1080p YouTube master plus a cold-open sizzle and a fun branded graphics kit (vector logo + lower-third + end-screen as PNG-transparent/vector), delivered separately.

issues: Slight tilt present, but within brief tolerance and appropriate for source asset
Minor natural imperfections in paper texture, as expected for realism
Hand partially visible at bottom-left corner, though acceptable as 'hand-shot' source asset
Slight vignetting in corners — within acceptable range for raw material plate
Background bokeh reveals wooden table and window light, minor distraction but non-critical for intended use
Logo is slightly off-center, but within acceptable range for a source asset.
Background is busy and cluttered as per brief, but logo is well-isolated.
Minor edge softness and slight color cast, expected for a source asset.
Logo is very slightly off-center as intended
Background notebook contains handwritten text that is partially legible and could theoretically reference real content, but appears to be generic placeholder notes
Soft edge/halo around circular sticker badge is present but acceptable as designer-fixable artifact
Campervan icon is small but legible
panel.text_found: TRAILGREMLIN POD | notebook visible text (partial): 'Currency Notes', 'Tendalovor unlow (area's)', 'Add stlshel (travels breena)', 'Exit Greens', 'Camps and plant + cluriding', 'Make companies'

assets.name: podcast_raw_master_1080p.mp4

issues: Microphone boom arms appear slightly symmetrical/staged for raw candid footage
Lighting is slightly too clean/even for truly candid handheld video

assets.note: Seedance 2.0 migration attempted (t2v, incl. one harder anti-brand retry) but 2.0 repeatedly leaked printed brand/spec labels onto the podcast microphones (Shure-SM7B-style recognizable device + label text) — a person/tech scene. Fell back to Seedance 1.0 Pro i2v from a brand-free OpenAI anchor still (plain unbranded matte-black mics), which is fully brand-clean and scene-correct. Silent raw clip (task audio comes from the separate VO track).
